design_thesis: This experience should feel calmly investigative and evidence-led for drivers dealing with a stressful total-loss offer because they come to decide whether the valuation is worth challenging; it must make the free review and paid independent appraisal immediately clear, prove who performs the work and what evidence the customer receives, and differ through real report artifacts, current state sources, and a document-review composition.

Mobile nav, homepage tabs, pricing disclosures, skip link, and form invalid focus tested in Chromium
3px #005fcc focus outline plus 6px white separation ring observed
One h1 and landmarks pass; pricing jumps from h1 to h3
axe serious color-contrast and link-in-text-block violations
Primary form has no in-page submitting, network error, retry, or success status
No running animations after load in reduced-motion contexts
320px text-size stress produced widths of 626px home, 344px pricing, and 388px free-review
Homepage scrollWidth 335px at a 320px viewport
Pricing adapts well, but internal pages largely stack and city pages put the form before local orientation
Owner confirmed on 2026-07-22 that the published homepage outcomes are real customer claims; identifying details remain redacted
Pattern ledger in audit report covers hero, pricing, FAQ, outcomes, card grids, and CTA bands
Adversarial critique completed
adversarial_critique_and_revision.reason: Audit-only scope did not authorize website revisions

evidence: axe-core 4.10.3 run on desktop and mobile primary plus representative content routes
Keyboard/focus/reflow pass completed; screen-reader pass not completed and form recovery fails

data_fetching.reason: Rendered page content is static and does not fetch application data

data_presence.reason: No stored or filterable dynamic data surface is present

evidence: Native invalid state passes; simulated network failure loses page and input
Buttons, tabs, details, form controls, hover, focus, and selection inspected
Current state, expanded/collapsed, Escape, and focus return verified
No customer-authorized vehicle photo is supplied for the outcome panel

auth.reason: No identity-dependent routes or controls exist

permissions.reason: No role or entitlement dependent capability exists

evidence: 320px and 200% text-size overflow
Reduced motion passes, but enlarged text and reflow fail

localization.reason: Current stated product scope is English-only service in the United States

pattern_justifications.pattern: Split task-first homepage hero with inline form
pattern_justifications.content_or_task_job: Explains the offer and lets urgent users begin a free review without another navigation step
pattern_justifications.context_fit: High-intent drivers may be time-sensitive and already have the insurer offer available
pattern_justifications.alternative_considered: One hero CTA to the dedicated free-review route
pattern_justifications.selection_reason: The split view makes price, timing, scope, and action simultaneously visible on desktop
pattern_justifications.constraints: Mobile orientation must precede the long form; loading, error, retry, success, keyboard, and privacy states are required
pattern_justifications.rendered_evidence: Homepage 1440x900 and 390x844 full-page captures plus form state tests

pattern_justifications.pattern: Pricing cards plus comparison table and mobile disclosures
pattern_justifications.content_or_task_job: Compares three parallel packages with shared fields and different support depth
pattern_justifications.context_fit: Users need transparent one-time price and deliverable differences before purchase
pattern_justifications.selection_reason: Cards support initial selection while the table supports detailed comparison; mobile disclosures avoid a squeezed table
pattern_justifications.constraints: Correct heading levels, long copy, keyboard disclosure, contrast, 320px, and 200% text-size behavior
pattern_justifications.rendered_evidence: Pricing desktop/mobile full-page captures and keyboard disclosure test

pattern_justifications.content_or_task_job: Reveals secondary purchase answers on demand
pattern_justifications.context_fit: Pricing objections are important but should not dominate first comparison
pattern_justifications.selection_reason: Native details preserve semantics and keyboard behavior with low implementation complexity
pattern_justifications.constraints: Answers that belong near a purchase decision should be moved there; summary targets must remain large and clear
pattern_justifications.rendered_evidence: Desktop/mobile pricing and guide captures with keyboard interaction

pattern_justifications.content_or_task_job: Intended to compare claim outcomes as social proof
pattern_justifications.context_fit: The owner-confirmed real cases answer whether the service has changed outcomes in particular matters
pattern_justifications.selection_reason: Retain the real customer outcomes with privacy redactions and individual-results language
pattern_justifications.constraints: Requires approved records, accurate context, keyboard operation, a narrow alternative, and zero overflow
pattern_justifications.rendered_evidence: Homepage full-page captures, keyboard tab test, 320px overflow diagnostics, and repository proof audit

declared_automatic_failures: No customer-authorized vehicle photo is supplied for the outcome panel
Primary lead form has no loading, network-error, preserved-input, or retry path; simulated failure loses the page and data
200% text-size stress causes horizontal overflow on home, pricing, and free-review
Serious WCAG contrast and link-distinction violations remain on primary and representative routes
